1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""Generate the CFO Briefing .docx — 2 pages, formatted per spec."""
3	
4	from docx import Document
5	from docx.shared import Pt, Cm, Emu, RGBColor
6	from docx.enum.text import WD_ALIGN_PARAGRAPH
7	from docx.enum.section import WD_ORIENT
8	from docx.enum.table import WD_TABLE_ALIGNMENT
9	from docx.oxml.ns import qn, nsdecls
10	from docx.oxml import parse_xml
11	import copy
12	
13	
14	def set_cell_shading(cell, color_hex):
15	    """Set cell background shading."""
16	    shading = parse_xml(f'<w:shd {nsdecls("w")} w:fill="{color_hex}"/>')
17	    cell._tc.get_or_add_tcPr().append(shading)
18	
19	
20	def set_paragraph_spacing(paragraph, space_before=0, space_after=0, line_spacing=1.15):
21	    """Set paragraph spacing."""
22	    pf = paragraph.paragraph_format
23	    pf.space_before = Pt(space_before)
24	    pf.space_after = Pt(space_after)
25	    pf.line_spacing = line_spacing
26	
27	
28	def add_bold_run(paragraph, text, font_size=12, font_name="Calibri"):
29	    """Add a bold run to a paragraph."""
30	    run = paragraph.add_run(text)
31	    run.bold = True
32	    run.font.size = Pt(font_size)
33	    run.font.name = font_name
34	    return run
35	
36	
37	def add_run(paragraph, text, bold=False, font_size=12, font_name="Calibri"):
38	    """Add a formatted run to a paragraph."""
39	    run = paragraph.add_run(text)
40	    run.bold = bold
41	    run.font.size = Pt(font_size)
42	    run.font.name = font_name
43	    return run
44	
45	
46	def format_table_cell(cell, text, bold=False, align=WD_ALIGN_PARAGRAPH.LEFT,
47	                       font_size=10, font_name="Calibri"):
48	    """Format a table cell with text."""
49	    cell.text = ""
50	    p = cell.paragraphs[0]
51	    p.alignment = align
52	    run = p.add_run(text)
53	    run.bold = bold
54	    run.font.size = Pt(font_size)
55	    run.font.name = font_name
56	    # Reduce cell paragraph spacing
57	    pf = p.paragraph_format
58	    pf.space_before = Pt(1)
59	    pf.space_after = Pt(1)

1	# REFERENCE LIBRARY
2	
3	> **Generated:** 2026-05-02
4	> **Coverage areas:** Modern portfolio theory, capital market assumptions, risk parity, liquidity premia, strategic vs tactical allocation, factor-based allocation
5	> **Citation style:** APA 7th
6	> **Total sources:** 20 (12 targeted, 8 standby)
7	> **Note:** Upstream findings documents are not yet available (all input paths are `None`). Targeted sources are matched to the typical analytical claims expected in an asset allocation brief on risk, return, and liquidity. Once the asset-class dataset and task description are provided, this library should be reviewed and supplemented with sources specific to any novel findings.
8	
9	---
10	
11	## Targeted Sources
12	Sources matched to expected findings in an asset allocation risk-return-liquidity brief
13	
14	### Markowitz, 1952
15	**Full Citation:** Markowitz, H. (1952). Portfolio selection. *The Journal of Finance*, *7*(1), 77–91. https://doi.org/10.2307/2975974
16	**Key Finding:** Investors should select portfolios based on the entire mean-variance efficient frontier rather than maximising expected return alone, formalising the risk-return tradeoff as the foundation of modern portfolio theory.
17	**Tags:** #modern-portfolio-theory | #mean-variance-optimization | #efficient-frontier
18	**Supports:** "Mean-variance optimisation identifies a set of efficient portfolios that maximise expected return for each level of risk."
19	
20	### DeMiguel, Garlappi, & Uppal, 2009
21	**Full Citation:** DeMiguel, V., Garlappi, L., & Uppal, R. (2009). Optimal versus naive diversification: How inefficient is the 1/N portfolio strategy? *The Review of Financial Studies*, *22*(5), 1915–1953. https://doi.org/10.1093/rfs/hhn048
22	**Key Finding:** The equally-weighted (1/N) portfolio consistently matches or outperforms optimised portfolios out-of-sample because estimation error in expected returns, variances, and covariances dominates any theoretical gains from optimisation.

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""Generate the CFO Briefing .docx — 2 pages, formatted per spec."""
3	
4	from docx import Document
5	from docx.shared import Pt, Cm, Emu, RGBColor
6	from docx.enum.text import WD_ALIGN_PARAGRAPH
7	from docx.enum.section import WD_ORIENT
8	from docx.enum.table import WD_TABLE_ALIGNMENT
9	from docx.oxml.ns import qn, nsdecls
10	from docx.oxml import parse_xml
11	import copy
12	
13	
14	def set_cell_shading(cell, color_hex):
15	    """Set cell background shading."""
16	    shading = parse_xml(f'<w:shd {nsdecls("w")} w:fill="{color_hex}"/>')
17	    cell._tc.get_or_add_tcPr().append(shading)
18	
19	
20	def set_paragraph_spacing(paragraph, space_before=0, space_after=0, line_spacing=1.15):
21	    """Set paragraph spacing."""
22	    pf = paragraph.paragraph_format
23	    pf.space_before = Pt(space_before)
24	    pf.space_after = Pt(space_after)
25	    pf.line_spacing = line_spacing
26	
27	
28	def add_bold_run(paragraph, text, font_size=12, font_name="Calibri"):
29	    """Add a bold run to a paragraph."""
30	    run = paragraph.add_run(text)
31	    run.bold = True
32	    run.font.size = Pt(font_size)
33	    run.font.name = font_name
34	    return run
35	
36	
37	def add_run(paragraph, text, bold=False, font_size=12, font_name="Calibri"):
38	    """Add a formatted run to a paragraph."""
39	    run = paragraph.add_run(text)
40	    run.bold = bold
41	    run.font.size = Pt(font_size)
42	    run.font.name = font_name
43	    return run
44	
45	
46	def format_table_cell(cell, text, bold=False, align=WD_ALIGN_PARAGRAPH.LEFT,
47	                       font_size=10, font_name="Calibri"):
48	    """Format a table cell with text."""
49	    cell.text = ""
50	    p = cell.paragraphs[0]
51	    p.alignment = align
52	    run = p.add_run(text)
53	    run.bold = bold
54	    run.font.size = Pt(font_size)
55	    run.font.name = font_name
56	    # Reduce cell paragraph spacing
57	    pf = p.paragraph_format
58	    pf.space_before = Pt(1)
59	    pf.space_after = Pt(1)

text:
The content brief has been written to:

```
agent-system/outputs/onepage_content_brief.md
```

Here's what the brief allocates:

| Section | Words | Marks | Depth | Min. Citations |
|---------|:-----:|:-----:|:-----:|:--------------:|
| **What** | 40–55 | 1.0% | SURFACE | 0 |
| **How** | 70–90 | 1.5% | MODERATE | ≥1 |
| **Why** | 250–290 | 7.0% | DEEP | ≥3 |
| **Total** | **~400** | **9.5%** | — | **≥4** |

**Key design decisions:**

1. **Why gets 68% of words and 74% of marks** — the brief structures it into 4 sub-headings (Macro regime → Asset class outlook → Unit trust logic → Allocation rationale) with exact bullet counts and word targets per sub-heading.

2. **All specific data points are pre-extracted** for the writing agent — CPI 2.7%, asset class forecasts (cash 3.8% through PE 13.4%), trust returns (STI 4.1% / MTG 7.9% / LTG 10.6%), portfolio metrics (8.7% return, 8.1% risk, 350 bps above hurdle).

3. **Logic checks are wired into rubric checklists** — the risk-return ordering must hold across all asset classes; global equity's inflation resilience (US pricing power) must be explicitly stated.

4. **Citation map specifies exactly where** each of the ≥4 citations must land — 0 in What, ≥1 in How's return bullet (RBA for hurdle), ≥2 in Why's macro sub-section (RBA + IMF/Fed), ≥1 in Why's asset class sub-section (Bloomberg/CMA provider).

5. **Trim priority is explicit**: if over 400 words → cut How first → then Why sub-section B descriptive bullets → never cut Why sub-sections A or D.
